_meta.ruleset: ORIGINAL SPI rules (Bruce's decision 2026-07-10: original, not Stafford 2018 fan revision)

sources.scan1975: literature/blue-and-gray/BGChickamauga.pdf — original 1975 folio: Standard Rules (4pp) + Chickamauga Exclusive Rules + counter sheet + CRT/TEC page (p8) [PRIMARY for 'original' questions]
sources.deluxe: literature/blue-and-gray/BlueGray_Deluxe_Rules.pdf — Decision Games Deluxe reprint: original rules + official errata (red) + play examples (blue). Standard 1.0-12.0 pp3-9; Chickamauga = section 18 pp26-28
sources.module: C:\VassalBlueGray\extracted — Blue and Gray Complete.vmod v1.0: Chickamauga Start.vsav, Chickamauga.png map, chart images
sources.stafford2018: literature/blue-and-gray/396181570-...pdf — fan revision, CLARIFICATION AID ONLY, changes the game (leaders/disruption/2d6 CRT/breastworks)

_meta.validation: CRT + TEC + deployment + reinforcements below verified IDENTICAL in scan1975 and deluxe unless a divergence is explicitly flagged in source_defect_candidates

movement.citation: [5.0] 'The Movement Allowance of all units is six Movement Points' (1975 p2; deluxe p4 'All units have a Movement Allowance of 6 MP')
movement.note_always_one_hex: NO minimum-one-hex rule in the original (that is Stafford 6.15 Exception). If terrain cost exceeds remaining MP the hex may not be entered [5.21/deluxe 5.2 cumulative note].
movement.road_citation: [5.22] road hex->adjacent road hex through road hexside = 1 MP regardless of other terrain
movement.trail_citation: [5.23] trail->trail through trail hexside = 2 MP if hex has forest and/or rough; clear = 1 MP. TEC: 'Ignore Trails in Clear Terrain' (Chickamauga only)
movement.ford_example: [5.25] ford hexside into forest hex = 4 MP total (1975); deluxe example identical
movement.river_hex: not on Chickamauga map (Shiloh/Antietam only per TEC)
movement.stacking_citation: [5.32] max two units regardless of strength or type; [5.31] free passage through friendly; [5.33] unlimited pass-through

zoc.entry_citation: [6.0] 'All units must cease movement when they enter an Enemy controlled hex'
zoc.exit_during_movement: PROHIBITED — [5.13]/[6.3]: exit ONLY by retreat/advance as combat result, or removal of the exerting enemy unit by combat. (Stafford's +1MP walk-out is HIS change; original/deluxe agree: no exit.)
zoc.blocked_hexsides: ZOCs never extend through non-bridge non-ford creek hexsides, nor into river ferry hexes [6.6 + diagram p2 1975; deluxe p5]. (Deluxe adds: DO extend across Stream hexsides — no streams on Chickamauga map.)

combat.mandatory: [7.0] combat between adjacent opposing units is mandatory
combat.all_adjacent_must_be_attacked: [7.11] every enemy unit with friendly units in its ZOC must be attacked; [7.12] every phasing unit ending movement in EZOC must attack; all friendly units adjacent to an enemy must participate
combat.multi_hex: [7.24] units in different hexes may combine against one hex if all adjacent (or artillery in range); [7.25] multi-defender combat requires ALL attackers adjacent to ALL defenders (bombarding artillery exempt)
combat.attackers_in_same_hex_integral: [7.22] (deluxe: 'Attacking units in the same hex must be used as a combined and unified Combat Strength')
combat.multi_zoc_obligation: [7.23] unit in ZOC of >1 enemy must attack all those not engaged by another attacker
combat.once_per_phase: [7.14] each unit attacks max once; each enemy attacked max once
combat.odds_rounding: down, in favor of defender: 13 vs 4 = 3.25:1 -> 3-1 [7.0 PROCEDURE]
combat.voluntary_odds_reduction: [7.9] attacker may announce lower odds column before rolling
combat.terrain_effect: [7.4] defender Combat Strength MULTIPLIED (doubled); NOT cumulative — best single benefit applies (deluxe 7.4 Important + 9.0 clarification)
combat.defense_doubling_hexside_rule: TEC + deluxe 9.0 clarification: defender doubled only if ALL adjacent attacking units attack across ford/bridge hexsides (participation of non-adjacent (bombarding) units has no effect on this)
combat.artillery_bombard_doubling_clarification: deluxe 9.0: unit attacked SOLELY by artillery is doubled if the LOS crosses a ford/bridge/stream hexside, a creek hexside, or an impassable hex [SPI official clarification]

crt.beyond_max: attacks > 6-1 treated as 6-1; worse than 1-5 treated as 1-5 [7.6 chart note]

results.Ae: Attacker Eliminated — all attacking units removed
results.De: Defender Eliminated — all defending units removed
results.Ex: Exchange — all defenders eliminated; attacker must eliminate participating attacking units whose total PRINTED strength >= total printed strength of eliminated defenders [7.6]
results.Ar: Attacker Retreats — all attacking units retreat one hex [7.7]
results.Dr: Defender Retreats — all defending units retreat one hex [7.7]

crt.validated: 1975 scan p8 == deluxe p5 text, all 60 cells, verified cell-by-cell 2026-07-10. Third source (module chart image) pending in validate_combat.py.

retreat_advance.retreat_one_hex_out_of_ezoc: [7.71] retreated units must end no longer in an EZOC; owner picks direction; two units same hex may split
retreat_advance.retreat_prohibitions: [7.72] no prohibited hex/hexside, no EZOC; if no hex open -> ELIMINATED
retreat_advance.retreat_stacking: [7.73] may join friendly stack (within stacking) if that stack not in EZOC; else may displace [7.8]
retreat_advance.retreated_unit_bombarded_later: [7.74] retreated-into-hex attacked by bombardment same phase: unit adds NO strength but suffers result
retreat_advance.advance: [7.75] one victorious participating unit may advance into vacated hex, ignores EZOC, immediate, optional, max 1 hex; advanced units may neither attack nor be attacked that phase
retreat_advance.advance_single: [7.76] only ONE unit advances even if two hexes vacated
retreat_advance.displacement: [7.81] retreating unit may displace ONE friendly unit if no other retreat path; displaced unit retreats likewise (chains allowed [7.82]); displaced-to-elimination -> retreating unit eliminated instead; displaced artillery not yet fired may not fire this phase; 1-for-1 in stacks

artillery.range_count: include target hex, exclude own hex [8.12]
artillery.adjacent_must_fight_normally: [8.41] in EZOC artillery must join normal attack, may NOT bombard; treated as infantry
artillery.bombarding_immune: [8.15] bombarding artillery suffers no combat results; [8.16] MAY voluntarily take Ar
artillery.combined: [8.21-8.23] may add bombarding strength to adjacent friendly attacks; in multi-hex attacks needs range to only one defending hex; adjacent cooperating units suffer results, bombarding artillery does not
artillery.los: [8.31-8.35] straight edge center-to-center; blocking = Forest, Forest&Rough, Town hexes (NOT rough alone); hexside-congruent LOS blocked only if BOTH adjacent hexes block; target + firer hexes never block; may fire over units
artillery.defense: [8.51] may never add strength to another unit's defense; [8.52] when attacked/bombarded suffers results normally
artillery.creek_exception: [8.45] artillery across a creek hexside from its only adjacent enemy MAY bombard (ZOC doesn't cross creek) — may bombard any target in range incl. that adjacent unit

night_turns.combat: [10.1] no combat phase, no combat of any kind, no bombardment
night_turns.movement: [10.2] normal restrictions PLUS: may not ENTER EZOC; units already in EZOC may NOT exit (original+deluxe; Stafford allowed paid exit — his change)

attack_effectiveness_optional_11_0.status: OPTIONAL rule in original AND deluxe. NOT enforced at launch — declared umpired/off; candidate future option. Designer notes say it was 'originally an organic part of the game'.
attack_effectiveness_optional_11_0.summary: Ar result flips attacker(s) face-down = Ineffective (attack from EZOC only [11.1]); may not attack nor enter EZOC; defends normally, keeps ZOC [11.2]; if in EZOC at own combat phase start must retreat per 7.7 unless enemy retreated away [11.31]; recovery: end of any Night GT flips all face-up [11.4]

chickamauga_exclusive.deployment_citation: 1975 scan p5 [14.1]/[14.2] — ORIGINAL chart (Stafford's differing strengths/generals are HIS edits; deluxe PACs not in rules PDF)
chickamauga_exclusive.reinforcement_rules: [15.0] enter on southern edge at listed hex during owner's Movement Phase, column formation: 1st unit 1 MP to enter, 2nd 2 MP, 3rd 3 MP...; may enter EZOC; if both entry hexes physically occupied -> delayed [15.5]; never arrive stacked [15.4]; excess rolls to later GTs [15.3]
chickamauga_exclusive.exit_rules: [16.x] exit costs 1 MP; exited = removed forever, NOT eliminated for enemy VP; no exit as retreat (eliminated instead); unlimited units per exit hex

line_of_communications.confederate: [17.31] CSA exit VPs require an end-of-game road trace from 0101 or 0110 (deluxe: 0111) off EASTERN edge, free of Union UNITS (ZOCs OK)
line_of_communications.union: [17.32] Union units (incl. blocked reinforcements) unable to trace a path of <=10 hexes to a road exiting at 0101/0110 (deluxe 0111) at game end = destroyed for VP purposes; path may cross EZOC, not enemy units

train_unit.auto_retreat: [18.11] adjacent to CSA in UNION combat phase -> must retreat (CSA may not advance after)
train_unit.stacking: [18.21/18.22] never stacks; NO unit may move through its hex; it may not move through units
train_unit.movement: [18.23] MA 6, roads/trails ONLY; forced retreat to non-road/trail hex = destroyed
train_unit.zoc: [18.25] none; CSA not required to attack it

module_deviations_worksheet.evidence: Chickamauga Start.vsav places Wilder at 0822 (chart: 1022) and omits 2/4/XIV (5 CSP, chart hex 0822) from the initial setup entirely. All other 45 at-start units match the 1975 deployment chart exactly (32/32 CSA, 13/14 Union).
module_deviations_worksheet.resolution: Our scenario follows the printed 1975 chart: Wilder at 1022, 2/4/XIV at 0822.
module_deviations_worksheet.status: RESOLVED (worksheet entry; not a source_defects item)

grid_solution.formula: col = round((x-7)/95.1); row = round((y+12)/120) if col odd else round((y-48)/120); hex = f'{col:02d}{row:02d}'
grid_solution.validated: 45/45 module at-start positions resolve to chart hexes under this formula (the 2 deviations above are name/omission issues, not position math)
grid_solution.map_image: C:\VassalBlueGray\extracted\images\Chickamauga.png 2737x3532, flat-top, col-stagger (odd cols up), playable cols 01-26, rows to 28 (jagged), TRT + reinforcement boxes printed at cols 27-28 (off-play)

source_defect_candidates.kind: rules/map mismatch in original printed game
source_defect_candidates.evidence: 1975 rules [16.1]/[17.31]/[17.32] say exit hexes '0101 and 0110' (three times); the original 1975 MAP shows the McFarland's Gap road exiting the west edge through 0111 — hex 0110 is clear terrain with NO road (verified from map scan 2026-07-10). 16.1's own text: 'units are presumed to move along the roads leading off the map'. Module redraw agrees (road in 0111). Deluxe 18.3/18.4.3 corrected to '0101 and 0111'.
source_defect_candidates.resolution: Exit hexes = 0101 and 0111 (the road hexes). LOC traces per 17.31/17.32 use 0101/0111.
source_defect_candidates.authority: original map evidence + Decision Games Deluxe correction (official errata-bearing edition)

source_defect_candidates.evidence: 1975 counter sheet (scan p9) CSA artillery counters read 3/4/5 (names 1,2,3); deployment chart gives arty '3'=5 CSP at 2120, reinforcement arty '2'=4, '1'=3 — consistent once matched by name not sheet order
source_defect_candidates.resolution: encode by chart values; verify module counter stats agree

credits_data.systems_design_development: Irad B. Hardy, III; John Michael Young
credits_data.production: Manfred F. Milkuhn, Larry Catalano, Kevin Zucker, Linda Mosca
credits_data.deluxe_reprint: Decision Games Blue & Gray Deluxe Edition (source of errata/clarifications)
credits_data.citation: 1975 scan p8 DESIGN CREDITS; deluxe p26 ORIGINAL DESIGN CREDITS (identical)
